title: API Initialization Pattern
description: Detailed guide on RTK Query architecture (Code Splitting).
invariant_text: It is forbidden to use <0>createApi</0> inside business modules of entities. The project uses a single caching core, divided into configuration and authorization layers. Features and entities "inject" their endpoints into the authorized core.

tags.title: Global Tag Registry (Shared Layer)
tags.description: For correct operation of invalidation <0>providesTags / invalidatesTags</0>, all possible tags are collected in a centralized config.

base.title: Base API Instance (Shared Layer)
base.description: The lowest level of network requests. No interceptors, only pure <0>fetchBaseQuery</0> and global tag registration.

auth_query.title: Authorization Interceptor (Entities Layer)
auth_query.description: A wrapper (Middleware) over the base request. It allows centralized handling of session loss (e.g., error 401) and executing <0>logout()</0> for the entire application.

auth_api.title: Authorized API Instance (Entities Layer)
auth_api.description: Initialization of a protected API instance (usually exported from the <0>auth</0> entity) using a custom <0>authBaseQuery</0>.

inject.title: Endpoint Injection (Entities Layer)
inject.description: The final stage in the target entity. We import <0>authApi</0> and call <0>injectEndpoints</0>. This code becomes the public service of the entity.
